[Source 1] doc_id=FAQ_CLAIM_108 | date=2024-01-01 | domain=pf
Q: Who allots the Universal Account Number? A: The UAN is allotted by EPFO on the request of the Employer and populated in the Employer's login at the unified portal www.unifiedportal-emp.epfindia.gov.in. The UAN can also be generated by any individual using their Aadhaar if UAN is not already generated.

---

[Source 2] doc_id=PASSBOOK_AND_INTEREST | date=2024-01-01 | domain=pf
EPF Passbook shows monthly contributions, transfer credits, and interest credited.
        
        HOW TO ACCESS PASSBOOK:
        - Login at https://unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in > View > Passbook > Enter UAN, password, captcha
        - Direct access: https://passbook.epfindia.gov.in/MemberPassBook/Login
        - Download: Go to Download menu > Download Passbook > PDF download link available
        - Via UMANG App > EPFO > Employee Centric Services > Passbook
        - Via missed call to 011-22901406
        - Via SMS to 7738299899 in 10 languages
        
        PASSBOOK NOT AVAILABLE:
        If working in an Exempted establishment, passbook is not available on UAN portal. Contact establishment directly for PF statement.
        
        INTEREST CREDITING:
        - Interest is credited on monthly running balance basis
        - Credited annually after Central Government declares rate
        - Delay in passbook update does not cause financial loss — interest is added to closing balance and becomes opening balance for next year
        - If member withdraws before interest is updated, due interest is auto-calculated and paid at claim settlement
        
        SERVICE HISTORY:
        Login to e-Passbook > Click on Service History to see employment details across multiple establishments.
        
        CLAIM STATUS:
        Track claim status at https://passbook.epfindia.gov.in/MemClaimStatusUAN/

prompt:
USER QUERY:
Left my job 2 months back, 8 years service. UAN active, KYC complete. Full PF withdrawal karna hai.

DOMAIN: pf

RETRIEVED PASSAGES:
[Source 1] doc_id=NEWS_EPF_CLAIM_REJECTION_REASONS_2024 | date=2024-01-01 | domain=pf
Common EPF claim rejection reasons and resolutions compiled from EPFO field office reports and member portal data. TOP 10 CLAIM REJECTION REASONS: 1. BANK ACCOUNT NOT VERIFIED / IFSC INCORRECT Fix: Re-enter bank details in KYC section. Get current IFSC from bank. Wait for employer approval. 2. DATE OF EXIT NOT UPDATED Fix: If 60+ days since leaving — self-update via Manage > Mark Exit. If less than 60 days — ask employer to update in their portal. 3. NAME MISMATCH (Aadhaar vs PF records) Fix: Submit Joint Declaration for name correction. Employer approves. Then re-link Aadhaar. 4. MEMBER ALREADY SETTLED (Duplicate claim attempt) Fix: Check passbook — if first claim was settled, the amount should be in your bank. If not, file grievance. 5. UAN NOT AADHAAR SEEDED Fix: Link Aadhaar via member portal > Manage > KYC > Aadhaar. Wait for UIDAI verification. 6. CLAIM FOR WRONG PURPOSE (e.g. Form 19 filed while still employed) Fix: Wait 2 months after leaving service, then file again. 7. INSUFFICIENT SERVICE FOR ADVANCE TYPE Fix: Check eligibility table. Select correct advance purpose matching your service period. 8. DOJ (Date of Joining) NOT UPDATED OR INCORRECT Fix: Contact employer to update DOJ via employer portal. Raise JD request if employer is unavail

[Source 2] doc_id=CIRC_ADVANCE_MEDICAL | date=2020-03-28 | domain=pf
COVID-19 / EPIDEMIC (Para 68-L): Non-refundable advance available once during declared epidemic. Amount: 75% of PF balance or 3 months' wages/DA, whichever is less.

---

[Source 3] doc_id=TDS_PRACTICAL_GUIDE_2024_chunk_1 | date=2024-04-01 | domain=pf
(basic exemption for senior citizens). 2024 UPDATES — KEY CHANGES: 1. TDS on Transfer NOT applicable: Transfer of PF from one account to another is not treated as withdrawal — no TDS even if service < 5 years. 2. Advances/Partial withdrawals (Form 31): TDS is NOT applicable on partial withdrawal advances. 3. Split accounts (EPF + VPF): TDS calculated on total taxable withdrawal, not split separately. 4. Non-resident (NRI) members: TDS at 30% (or DTAA rate if lower) — Form 15G/15H not available to NRIs. File Form 10F for DTAA benefit. WHAT HAPPENS AFTER TDS DEDUCTION: - Form 16A (TDS certificate) is issued by EPFO within 15 days from due date of TDS return - TDS can be claimed as credit in your Income Tax Return (ITR) - If your total income is below taxable limit, you can claim TDS refund from Income Tax Department EXAMPLE SCENARIOS: Scenario A: Service 4 years, balance Rs.80,000, valid PAN → TDS = 10% of Rs.80,000 = Rs.8,000. Submit Form 15G to avoid if income < Rs.2.5 lakh. Scenario B: Service 4 years, balance Rs.40,000, valid PAN → NO TDS (below Rs.50,000 threshold). Scenario C: Service 6 years, balance Rs.2,00,000 → NO TDS (service >= 5 years). Scenario D: Service 3 years, no PAN → TDS = 20% on amount above threshold. TAX YEAR NOTE: For withdrawal spanning two financial years, TDS is calculated in the year of withdrawal. Always provide latest Form 15G for the current financial year (April to March).

---

[Source 4] doc_id=CIRC_2024_TDS | date=2024-02-01 | domain=pf
TDS applicable if service < 5 years AND withdrawal exceeds Rs.50,000. Resident rate with valid PAN: 10%. Resident without valid PAN: 20%. Non-resident: 30% plus 4% cess. No TDS if service >= 5 years or transfer of funds.

[Source 1] doc_id=CASE_BHAVNA_BHATT_DELHI_HC_2018 | date=2018-01-01 | domain=pf
Case: Bhavna Bhatt v. Regional Provident Fund Commissioner (Delhi High Court, 2018). Relevance: Governs death claim process when no valid nomination exists. FACTS: Member died without having filed Form 2 nomination. Widow approached EPFO for PF settlement. EPFO demanded succession certificate from civil court, causing delay. HELD: Legal heir certificate from competent authority (Tehsildar / Revenue Officer / SDM) or court affidavit is sufficient for PF claim settlement when no nomination exists. EPFO cannot insist on succession certificate from civil court in all cases — this causes undue hardship to families. PROCESS FOR DEATH CLAIM WITHOUT NOMINATION: 1. Widow/widower can claim using legal heir certificate from SDM/Tehsildar 2. Children can claim with their birth certificates + death certificate of member 3. EPFO processes under Para 70(ii) of EPF Scheme — equal shares among family members 4. EPS family pension: Automatically payable to spouse. Submit Form 10D. 5. EDLI benefit: Form 5-IF. Same documents. DOCUMENTS NEEDED FOR DEATH CLAIM: - Death certificate of member - Legal heir certificate (SDM/Tehsildar) OR succession certificate from court if no legal heir cert available - Bank account details of claimant - Aadhaar of claimant (for UAN-linked accounts) -

FACTS: Member died without having filed Form 2 nomination. Widow approached EPFO for PF settlement. EPFO demanded succession certificate from civil court, causing delay. HELD: Legal heir certificate from competent authority (Tehsildar / Revenue Officer / SDM) or court affidavit is sufficient for PF claim settlement when no nomination exists. EPFO cannot insist on succession certificate from civil court in all cases — this causes undue hardship to families. PROCESS FOR DEATH CLAIM WITHOUT NOMINATION: 1. Widow/widower can claim using legal heir certificate from SDM/Tehsildar 2. Children can claim with their birth certificates + death certificate of member 3. EPFO processes under Para 70(ii) of EPF Scheme — equal shares among family members 4. EPS family pension: Automatically payable to spouse. Submit Form 10D. 5. EDLI benefit: Form 5-IF. Same documents. DOCUMENTS NEEDED FOR DEATH CLAIM: - Death certificate of member - Legal heir certificate (SDM/Tehsildar) OR succession certificate from court if no legal heir cert available - Bank account details of claimant - Aadhaar of claimant (for UAN-linked accounts) - Age proof of widow/children - Form 20 (EPF), Form 10D (EPS pension), Form 5-IF (EDLI)

Another Composite Form: Replaces Forms 20, 10D, 5-IF for death/pension cases.

TRANSFER FORMS:
Form 13 / Form 13(R): PF transfer request from previous to current account. Online preferred; physical only for exempted-to-exempted.
Annexure-K: Transfer from exempted trust — details sent by trust to EPFO/receiving trust.

MEMBER REGISTRATION / KYC:
Form 11 (New): Declaration of previous EPF service on new joining. Enables auto-transfer.
Form 2: Nomination form for EPF, EPS, and EDLI. Family

[Source 2] doc_id=NEWS_EPF_CLAIM_REJECTION_REASONS_2024 | date=2024-01-01 | domain=pf
Common EPF claim rejection reasons and resolutions compiled from EPFO field office reports and member portal data. TOP 10 CLAIM REJECTION REASONS: 1. BANK ACCOUNT NOT VERIFIED / IFSC INCORRECT Fix: Re-enter bank details in KYC section. Get current IFSC from bank. Wait for employer approval. 2. DATE OF EXIT NOT UPDATED Fix: If 60+ days since leaving — self-update via Manage > Mark Exit. If less than 60 days — ask employer to update in their portal. 3. NAME MISMATCH (Aadhaar vs PF records) Fix: Submit Joint Declaration for name correction. Employer approves. Then re-link Aadhaar. 4. MEMBER ALREADY SETTLED (Duplicate claim attempt) Fix: Check passbook — if first claim was settled, the amount should be in your bank. If not, file grievance. 5. UAN NOT AADHAAR SEEDED Fix: Link Aadhaar via member portal > Manage > KYC > Aadhaar. Wait for UIDAI verification. 6. CLAIM FOR WRONG PURPOSE (e.g. Form 19 filed while still employed) Fix: Wait 2 months after leaving service, then file again. 7. INSUFFICIENT SERVICE FOR ADVANCE TYPE Fix: Check eligibility table. Select correct advance purpose matching your service period. 8. DOJ (Date of Joining) NOT UPDATED OR INCORRECT Fix: Contact employer to update DOJ via employer portal. Raise JD request if employer is unavailable. 9. PAN NOT LINKED (causes high TDS issue) Fix: Link PAN in member portal > Manage > KYC > PAN. Employer approval needed. 10. ACTIVE CLAIM ALREADY PENDING (Can't file when claim in process) Fix: Wait for current claim to be se

[Source 1] doc_id=INOPERATIVE_ACCOUNTS | date=2024-01-01 | domain=pf
An EPF account is classified as Inoperative when no contribution has been received for 3 years after:
        - Retirement
        - Permanent migration abroad
        - Death
        
        INTEREST ON INOPERATIVE ACCOUNTS:
        - At present, all accounts earn interest up to age 58 of the member
        - After account becomes inoperative, no further interest is credited
        - Account becomes inoperative after 36 months from date of retirement (55 years+) or permanent migration
        
        WHAT TO DO WITH INOPERATIVE ACCOUNT:
        - If still employed in covered establishment: Get amount transferred to new account (online or offline Form 13)
        - If retired: Withdraw the amount by submitting withdrawal claim
        
        UNCLAIMED PF (SENIOR CITIZENS FUND):
        Unclaimed EPF amounts are transferred to Senior Citizens Welfare Fund (SCWF) after 7 years of becoming inoperative. However, members can still claim this amount from EPFO.
        
        HOW TO CLAIM INOPERATIVE ACCOUNT:
        - Trace the account using establishment name or PF number
        - Contact the employer for details
        - Submit withdrawal claim to concerned Regional PF Office with proper KYC documentation
        
        PASSBOOK FOR INOPERATIVE ACCOUNTS:

        - Contact the employer for details
        - Submit withdrawal claim to concerned Regional PF Office with proper KYC documentation

Practical guide on TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) for PF withdrawals, reflecting rules effective from April 2022 and updated in 2024. THE SIMPLE RULE — TWO CONDITIONS MUST BOTH BE TRUE FOR TDS: Condition 1: Total service is LESS than 5 years (counting service across all employers if PF was transferred) AND Condition 2: Withdrawal amount is MORE than Rs.50,000 If EITHER condition is false → NO TDS is deducted. COUNTING THE 5 YEARS: - Service includes all employers where PF was contributed AND transferred to your current account - Example: 3 years at Company A (transferred) + 2.5 years at Company B = 5.5 years total → NO TDS - If you did NOT transfer PF from previous employer, each account is treated separately - TDS applies per withdrawal claim, not per employer TDS RATES (for residential Indians with PAN): Scenario | TDS Rate PAN valid + service < 5 yrs + > 50k | 10% No valid PAN + service < 5 yrs + >50k | 20% Service 5+ years (regardless of amount)| 0% Amount ≤ Rs.50,000 | 0% HOW TO SUBMIT FORM 15G (to get zero TDS): WHO: Resident individual below 60 years whose estimated total income for the year is below Rs.2,50,000 (i.e., below basic exemption limit). WHEN: Submit BEFORE filing the withdrawal claim. Cannot be submitted after TDS is deducted. WHERE (Online): Login t

[Source 1] doc_id=TDS_PRACTICAL_GUIDE_2024 | date=2024-04-01 | domain=pf
Practical guide on TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) for PF withdrawals, reflecting rules effective from April 2022 and updated in 2024. THE SIMPLE RULE — TWO CONDITIONS MUST BOTH BE TRUE FOR TDS: Condition 1: Total service is LESS than 5 years (counting service across all employers if PF was transferred) AND Condition 2: Withdrawal amount is MORE than Rs.50,000 If EITHER condition is false → NO TDS is deducted. COUNTING THE 5 YEARS: - Service includes all employers where PF was contributed AND transferred to your current account - Example: 3 years at Company A (transferred) + 2.5 years at Company B = 5.5 years total → NO TDS - If you did NOT transfer PF from previous employer, each account is treated separately - TDS applies per withdrawal claim, not per employer TDS RATES (for residential Indians with PAN): Scenario | TDS Rate PAN valid + service < 5 yrs + > 50k | 10% No valid PAN + service < 5 yrs + >50k | 20% Service 5+ years (regardless of amount)| 0% Amount ≤ Rs.50,000 | 0% HOW TO SUBMIT FORM 15G (to get zero TDS): WHO: Resident individual below 60 years whose estimated total income for the year is below Rs.2,50,000 (i.e., below basic exemption limit). WHEN: Submit BEFORE filing the withdrawal claim. Cannot be submitted after TDS is deducted. WHERE (Online): Login to member portal > Online Services > Claim > You'll see a checkbox/link to upload Form 15G before submitting the claim. Upload 2 copies. WHERE (Offline): Submit 2 physical copies along with claim form to PF office.

Another Composite Form: Replaces Forms 20, 10D, 5-IF for death/pension cases.

TRANSFER FORMS:
Form 13 / Form 13(R): PF transfer request from previous to current account. Online preferred; physical only for exempted-to-exempted.
Annexure-K: Transfer from exempted trust — details sent by trust to EPFO/receiving trust.

MEMBER REGISTRATION / KYC:
Form 11 (New): Declaration of previous EPF service on new joining. Enables auto-transfer.
Form 2: Nomination form for EPF, EPS, and EDLI. Family and nominee details.
Form 3A/ECR Copy: Contribution card. Member can demand from employer.

TAX FORMS:
Form 15G: Declaration for zero TDS if total income below Rs.2.5 lakh (non-senior citizens, any age). Needs PAN

DOMAIN: pf

[Source 1] doc_id=TDS_PRACTICAL_GUIDE_2024 | date=2024-04-01 | domain=pf
Practical guide on TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) for PF withdrawals, reflecting rules effective from April 2022 and updated in 2024. THE SIMPLE RULE — TWO CONDITIONS MUST BOTH BE TRUE FOR TDS: Condition 1: Total service is LESS than 5 years (counting service across all employers if PF was transferred) AND Condition 2: Withdrawal amount is MORE than Rs.50,000 If EITHER condition is false → NO TDS is deducted. COUNTING THE 5 YEARS: - Service includes all employers where PF was contributed AND transferred to your current account - Example: 3 years at Company A (transferred) + 2.5 years at Company B = 5.5 years total → NO TDS - If you did NOT transfer PF from previous employer, each account is treated separately - TDS applies per withdrawal claim, not per employer TDS RATES (for residential Indians with PAN): Scenario | TDS Rate PAN valid + service < 5 yrs + > 50k | 10% No valid PAN + service < 5 yrs + >50k | 20% Service 5+ years (regardless of amount)| 0% Amount ≤ Rs.50,000 | 0% HOW TO SUBMIT FORM 15G (to get zero TDS): WHO: Resident individual below 60 years whose estimated total income for the year is below Rs.2,50,000 (i.e., below basic exemption limit). WHEN: Submit BEFORE filing the withdrawal claim. Cannot be submitted after TDS is deducted. WHERE (Online): Log

Practical guide on TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) for PF withdrawals, reflecting rules effective from April 2022 and updated in 2024. THE SIMPLE RULE — TWO CONDITIONS MUST BOTH BE TRUE FOR TDS: Condition 1: Total service is LESS than 5 years (counting service across all employers if PF was transferred) AND Condition 2: Withdrawal amount is MORE than Rs.50,000 If EITHER condition is false → NO TDS is deducted. COUNTING THE 5 YEARS: - Service includes all employers where PF was contributed AND transferred to your current account - Example: 3 years at Company A (transferred) + 2.5 years at Company B = 5.5 years total → NO TDS - If you did NOT transfer PF from previous employer, each account is treated separately - TDS applies per withdrawal claim, not per employer TDS RATES (for residential Indians with PAN): Scenario | TDS Rate PAN valid + service < 5 yrs + > 50k | 10% No valid PAN + service < 5 yrs + >50k | 20% Service 5+ years (regardless of amount)| 0% Amount ≤ Rs.50,000 | 0% HOW TO SUBMIT FORM 15G (to get zero TDS): WHO: Resident individual below 60 years whose estimated total income for the year is below Rs.2,50,000 (i.e., below basic exemption limit). WHEN: Submit BEFORE filing the withdrawal claim. Cannot be submitted after TDS is deducted. WHERE (Online): Login to member portal > Online Services > Claim > You'll see a checkbox/link to upload Form 15G before submitting the claim. Upload 2 copies. WHERE (Offline): Submit 2 physical copies along with claim form to PF office.

---

[Source 2] doc_id=WD_FORM31_PARTIAL_PRACTICAL | date=2024-01-01 | domain=pf
Form 31 is used for non-refundable advance / partial withdrawal from EPF for specific approved purposes. This is different from full withdrawal (Form 19). WHEN TO USE FORM 31 (not Form 19): - You are still employed OR recently unemployed (less than 2 months) - You need money for illness, marriage, education, housing, or pre-retirement (not full settlement) - You want to withdraw up to 75% while unemployed for more than 1 month but less than 2 months ELIGIBILITY QUICK REFERENCE BY PURPOSE: Purpose | Min Service | Max Amount | Allowed Times Illness | None | 6 months wages or employee's share (lesser) | No limit Marriage | 7 years | 50% of employee's share | Max 3 times Education | 7 years | 50% of employee's share | Max 3 times Housing purchase| 5 years | 24 months wages or PF balance (lesser) | Once Loan repayment | 10 years | 36 months wages or PF balance (lesser) | Once Pre-retirement | 54+ age | 90% of total PF balance | Once Unemployment | Any | 75% of total PF balance | Once (for 1-2 months unemployed) HOW TO FILE FORM 31 ONLINE (Step-by-Step): Step 1: Login to https://unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in/memberinterface/ → Enter UAN, password, and captcha. Step 2: Verify KYC is complete. → Go to Manage > KYC. Confirm bank account shows 'Digitally Approved by Employer' and Aadhaar shows 'Verified by UIDAI'. If not, complete KYC first. Step 3: Navigate to Online Services > Claim (Form-31, 19, 10C & 10D). Step 4: Verify bank account. → Your KYC-approved bank account appears. E

(basic exemption for senior citizens). 2024 UPDATES — KEY CHANGES: 1. TDS on Transfer NOT applicable: Transfer of PF from one account to another is not treated as withdrawal — no TDS even if service < 5 years. 2. Advances/Partial withdrawals (Form 31): TDS is NOT applicable on partial withdrawal advances. 3. Split accounts (EPF + VPF): TDS calculated on total taxable withdrawal, not split separately. 4. Non-resident (NRI) members: TDS at 30% (or DTAA rate if lower) — Form 15G/15H not available to NRIs. File Form 10F for DTAA benefit. WHAT HAPPENS AFTER TDS DEDUCTION: - Form 16A (TDS certificate) is issued by EPFO within 15 days from due date of TDS return - TDS can be claimed as credit in your Income Tax Return (ITR) - If your total income is below taxable limit, you can claim TDS refund from Income Tax Department EXAMPLE SCENARIOS: Scenario A: Service 4 years, balance Rs.80,000, valid PAN → TDS = 10% of Rs.80,000 = Rs.8,000. Scenario C: Service 6 years, balance Rs.2,00,000 → NO TDS (service >= 5 years). Scenario D: Service 3 years, no PAN → TDS = 20% on amount above threshold. prompt:
USER QUERY:
Resigned 3 months ago after 5 years service. UAN active, KYC partial. Can I withdraw full PF?

DOMAIN: pf

RETRIEVED PASSAGES:
[Source 1] doc_id=CASE_BHAVNA_BHATT_DELHI_HC_2018 | date=2018-01-01 | domain=pf
Case: Bhavna Bhatt v. Regional Provident Fund Commissioner (Delhi High Court, 2018). Relevance: Governs death claim process when no valid nomination exists. FACTS: Member died without having filed Form 2 nomination. Widow approached EPFO for PF settlement. EPFO demanded succession certificate from civil court, causing delay. HELD: Legal heir certificate from competent authority (Tehsildar / Revenue Officer / SDM) or court affidavit is sufficient for PF claim settlement when no nomination exists. EPFO cannot insist on succession certificate from civil court in all cases — this causes undue hardship to families. PROCESS FOR DEATH CLAIM WITHOUT NOMINATION: 1. Widow/widower can claim using legal heir certificate from SDM/Tehsildar 2. Children can claim with their birth certificates + death certificate of member 3. EPFO processes under Para 70(ii) of EPF Scheme — equal shares among family members 4. EPS family pension: Automatically payable to spouse. Submit Form 10D. 5. EDLI benefit: Form 5-IF. Same documents. DOCUMENTS NEEDED FOR DEATH CLAIM: - Death certificate of member - Legal heir certificate (SDM/Tehsildar) OR succession certificate from court if no legal heir cert available - Bank account details of claimant - Aadhaar of claimant (for UAN-linked accounts) -

Q: What are the benefits available to EPF members? A: Benefits include: (a) Advances for illness, marriage, education, housing, unemployment, natural calamity, physical handicap, pre-retirement; (b) Final settlement on retirement or 2 months after leaving service; (c) Pension after retirement subject to eligibility; (d) Insurance in case of death while in service.

[Source 3] doc_id=TDS_PRACTICAL_GUIDE_2024 | date=2024-04-01 | domain=pf
Practical guide on TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) for PF withdrawals, reflecting rules effective from April 2022 and updated in 2024. THE SIMPLE RULE — TWO CONDITIONS MUST BOTH BE TRUE FOR TDS: Condition 1: Total service is LESS than 5 years (counting service across all employers if PF was transferred) AND Condition 2: Withdrawal amount is MORE than Rs.50,000 If EITHER condition is false → NO TDS is deducted. COUNTING THE 5 YEARS: - Service includes all employers where PF was contributed AND transferred to your current account - Example: 3 years at Company A (transferred) + 2.5 years at Company B = 5.5 years total → NO TDS - If you did NOT transfer PF from previous employer, each account is treated separately - TDS applies per withdrawal claim, not per employer TDS RATES (for residential Indians with PAN): Scenario | TDS Rate PAN valid + service < 5 yrs + > 50k | 10% No valid PAN + service < 5 yrs + >50k | 20% Service 5+ years (regardless of amount)| 0% Amount ≤ Rs.50,000 | 0% HOW TO SUBMIT FORM 15G (to get zero TDS): WHO: Resident individual below 60 years whose estimated total income for the year is below Rs.2,50,000 (i.e., below basic exemption limit). WHEN: Submit BEFORE filing the withdrawal claim. Cannot be submitted after TDS is deducted. WHERE (Online): Login to member portal > Online Services > Claim > You'll see a checkbox/link to upload Form 15G before submitting the claim. Upload 2 copies. WHERE (Offline): Submit 2 physical copies along with claim form to PF office.

SO 1513(E) dated 18.05.2020 — Para 68L of EPF Scheme 1952 amended for COVID-19 relief. NON-REFUNDABLE ADVANCE PROVISION: - Available to ALL EPF members affected by COVID-19 pandemic - Amount: Lower of (a) 75% of total PF balance, or (b) 3 months' Basic Wages + DA - Can be withdrawn ONCE only (not available again) - No minimum service requirement - No documentation required to prove COVID impact - Available only when Government has declared epidemic/pandemic REDUCED CONTRIBUTION RATE (separate provision): - SO 1513(E) + SO 2120(E): EPF contribution rate reduced to 10% (from 12%) - For: All non-government establishments and non-public sector undertakings - For wage months: May 2020, June 2020, July 2020 - Both employee and employer contribution reduced to 10% - Government's contribution for PMGKY scheme continued at 12% STATUS AS OF 2024: This provision has lapsed. Members who availed COVID advance cannot avail again for the same account. HISTORICAL IMPORTANCE: If your passbook shows a claim from May-July 2020 for 75% advance, that was the COVID advance. [Source 2] doc_id=TDS_PRACTICAL_GUIDE_2024_chunk_1 | date=2024-04-01 | domain=pf
(basic exemption for senior citizens). 2024 UPDATES — KEY CHANGES: 1. TDS on Transfer NOT applicable: Transfer of PF from one account to another is not treated as withdrawal — no TDS even if service < 5 years. 2. Advances/Partial withdrawals (Form 31): TDS is NOT applicable on partial withdrawal advances. 3. Split accounts (EPF + VPF): TDS calculated on total taxable withdrawal, not split separately. 4. Non-resident (NRI) members: TDS at 30% (or DTAA rate if lower) — Form 15G/15H not available to NRIs. File Form 10F for DTAA benefit. WHAT HAPPENS AFTER TDS DEDUCTION: - Form 16A (TDS certificate) is issued by EPFO within 15 days from due date of TDS return - TDS can be claimed as credit in your Income Tax Return (ITR) - If your total income is below taxable limit, you can claim TDS refund from Income Tax Department EXAMPLE SCENARIOS: Scenario A: Service 4 years, balance Rs.80,000, valid PAN → TDS = 10% of Rs.80,000 = Rs.8,000. Submit Form 15G to avoid if income < Rs.2.5 lakh. Scenario B: Service 4 years, balance Rs.40,000, valid PAN → NO TDS (below Rs.50,000 threshold). Scenario C: Service 6 years, balance Rs.2,00,000 → NO TDS (service >= 5 years). Scenario D: Service 3 years, no PAN → TDS = 20% on amount above threshold. TAX YEAR NOTE: For withdrawal spanning two financial years, TDS is calculated in the year of withdrawal. Always provide latest Form 15G for the current financial year (April to March).

---

A: Benefits include: (a) Advances for illness, marriage, education, housing, unemployment, natural calamity, physical handicap, pre-retirement; (b) Final settlement on retirement or 2 months after leaving service; (c) Pension after retirement subject to eligibility; (d) Insurance in case of death while in service.

[Source 2] doc_id=CASE_BHAVNA_BHATT_DELHI_HC_2018 | date=2018-01-01 | domain=pf
Case: Bhavna Bhatt v. Regional Provident Fund Commissioner (Delhi High Court, 2018). Relevance: Governs death claim process when no valid nomination exists. FACTS: Member died without having filed Form 2 nomination. Widow approached EPFO for PF settlement. EPFO demanded succession certificate from civil court, causing delay. HELD: Legal heir certificate from competent authority (Tehsildar / Revenue Officer / SDM) or court affidavit is sufficient for PF claim settlement when no nomination exists. EPFO cannot insist on succession certificate from civil court in all cases — this causes undue hardship to families. PROCESS FOR DEATH CLAIM WITHOUT NOMINATION: 1. Widow/widower can claim using legal heir certificate from SDM/Tehsildar 2. Children can claim with their birth certificates + death certificate of member 3. EPFO processes under Para 70(ii) of EPF Scheme — equal shares among family members 4. EPS family pension: Automatically payable to spouse. Submit Form 10D. 5. EDLI benefit: Form 5-IF. Same documents. DOCUMENTS NEEDED FOR DEATH CLAIM: - Death certificate of member - Legal heir certificate (SDM/Tehsildar) OR succession certificate from court if no legal heir cert available - Bank account details of claimant - Aadhaar of claimant (for UAN-linked accounts) - Age proof of widow/children - Form 20 (EPF), Form 10D (EPS pension), Form 5-IF (EDLI)

---

}

2. Manage → KYC.
3. Update the mismatched document (Aadhaar/PAN/bank).
4. Wait for employer digital approval.

**For Aadhaar name mismatch:** Correct via UIDAI portal first, then re-seed.

Common EPF claim rejection reasons and resolutions compiled from EPFO field office reports and member portal data. TOP 10 CLAIM REJECTION REASONS: 1. BANK ACCOUNT NOT VERIFIED / IFSC INCORRECT Fix: Re-enter bank details in KYC section. Get current IFSC from bank. Wait for employer approval. 2. ACTIVE CLAIM ALREADY PENDING (Can't file when claim in process) Fix: Wait for current claim to be se

[Source 3] doc_id=WD_FORM31_PARTIAL_PRACTICAL | date=2024-01-01 | domain=pf
Form 31 is used for non-refundable advance / partial withdrawal from EPF for specific approved purposes. This is different from full withdrawal (Form 19). WHEN TO USE FORM 31 (not Form 19): - You are still employed OR recently unemployed (less than 2 months) - You need money for illness, marriage, education, housing, or pre-retirement (not full settlement) - You want to withdraw up to 75% while unemployed for more than 1 month but less than 2 months ELIGIBILITY QUICK REFERENCE BY PURPOSE: Purpose | Min Service | Max Amount | Allowed Times Illness | None | 6 months wages or employee's share (lesser) | No limit Marriage | 7 years | 50% of employee's share | Max 3 times Education | 7 years | 50% of employee's share | Max 3 times Housing purchase| 5 years | 24 months wages or PF balance (lesser) | Once Loan repayment | 10 years | 36 months wages or PF balance (lesser) | Once Pre-retirement | 54+ age | 90% of total PF balance | Once Unemployment | Any | 75% of total PF balance | Once (for 1-2 months unemployed) HOW TO FILE FORM 31 ONLINE (Step-by-Step): Step 1: Login to https://unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in/memberinterface/ → Enter UAN, password, and captcha. Step 2: Verify KYC is complete. → Go to Manage > KYC. Confirm bank account shows 'Digitally Approved by Employer' and Aadhaar shows 'Verified by UIDAI'. If not, complete KYC first. USER QUERY:

RETRIEVED PASSAGES:
[Source 1] doc_id=FAQ_WD_028 | date=2024-01-01 | domain=pf
Q: Does PF provide refundable loans for housing? A: No refundable loans are available. However, non-refundable advances for housing purposes are available under Para 68-B and related provisions of the EPF Scheme.

---

[Source 2] doc_id=EPF_SCHEME_PARA60_68 | date=1952-09-02 | domain=pf
Processing time: Claims are required to be settled within 20 working days as per EPF Scheme. UAN must be active and seeded with Aadhaar. KYC (Aadhaar and bank account) must be verified and approved by employer.

---

[Source 3] doc_id=TDS_PRACTICAL_GUIDE_2024_chunk_1 | date=2024-04-01 | domain=pf
(basic exemption for senior citizens). 2024 UPDATES — KEY CHANGES: 1. TDS on Transfer NOT applicable: Transfer of PF from one account to another is not treated as withdrawal — no TDS even if service < 5 years. 2. Advances/Partial withdrawals (Form 31): TDS is NOT applicable on partial withdrawal advances. 3. Split accounts (EPF + VPF): TDS calculated on total taxable withdrawal, not split separately. 4. Non-resident (NRI) members: TDS at 30% (or DTAA rate if lower) — Form 15G/15H not available to NRIs. File Form 10F for DTAA benefit. WHAT HAPPENS AFTER TDS DEDUCTION: - Form 16A (TDS certificate) is issued by EPFO within 15 days from due date of TDS return - TDS can be claimed as credit in your Income Tax Return (ITR) - If your total income is below taxable limit, you can claim TDS refund from Income Tax Department EXAMPLE SCENARIOS: Scenario A: Service 4 years, balance Rs.80,000, valid PAN → TDS = 10% of Rs.80,000 = Rs.8,000. Submit Form 15G to avoid if income < Rs.2.5 lakh. Scenario B: Service 4 years, balance Rs.40,000, valid PAN → NO TDS (below Rs.50,000 threshold). Scenario C: Service 6 years, balance Rs.2,00,000 → NO TDS (service >= 5 years). Scenario D: Service 3 years, no PAN → TDS = 20% on amount above threshold. TAX YEAR NOTE: For withdrawal spanning two financial years, TDS is calculated in the year of withdrawal. Always provide latest Form 15G for the current financial year (April to March).

---

TDS applicable if service < 5 years AND withdrawal exceeds Rs.50,000. Resident rate with valid PAN: 10%. Resident without valid PAN: 20%. Non-resident: 30% plus 4% cess. No TDS if service >= 5 years or transfer of funds.

[Source 1] doc_id=TDS_PRACTICAL_GUIDE_2024 | date=2024-04-01 | domain=pf
Practical guide on TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) for PF withdrawals, reflecting rules effective from April 2022 and updated in 2024. THE SIMPLE RULE — TWO CONDITIONS MUST BOTH BE TRUE FOR TDS: Condition 1: Total service is LESS than 5 years (counting service across all employers if PF was transferred) AND Condition 2: Withdrawal amount is MORE than Rs.50,000 If EITHER condition is false → NO TDS is deducted. COUNTING THE 5 YEARS: - Service includes all employers where PF was contributed AND transferred to your current account - Example: 3 years at Company A (transferred) + 2.5 years at Company B = 5.5 years total → NO TDS - If you did NOT transfer PF from previous employer, each account is treated separately - TDS applies per withdrawal claim, not per employer TDS RATES (for residential Indians with PAN): Scenario | TDS Rate PAN valid + service < 5 yrs + > 50k | 10% No valid PAN + service < 5 yrs + >50k | 20% Service 5+ years (regardless of amount)| 0% Amount ≤ Rs.50,000 | 0% HOW TO SUBMIT FORM 15G (to get zero TDS): WHO: Resident individual below 60 years whose estimated total income for the year is below Rs.2,50,000 (i.e., below basic exemption limit). WHEN: Submit BEFORE filing the withdrawal claim. Cannot be submitted after TDS is deducted. WHERE (Online): Login to member portal > Online Services > Claim > You'll see a checkbox/link to upload Form 15G before submitting the claim. Upload 2 copies. WHERE (Offline): Submit 2 physical copies along with claim form to PF office.

---

[Source 2] doc_id=CASE_BHAVNA_BHATT_DELHI_HC_2018 | date=2018-01-01 | domain=pf
Case: Bhavna Bhatt v. Regional Provident Fund Commissioner (Delhi High Court, 2018). Relevance: Governs death claim process when no valid nomination exists. FACTS: Member died without having filed Form 2 nomination. Widow approached EPFO for PF settlement. EPFO demanded succession certificate from civil court, causing delay. HELD: Legal heir certificate from competent authority (Tehsildar / Revenue Officer / SDM) or court affidavit is sufficient for PF claim settlement when no nomination exists. EPFO cannot insist on succession certificate from civil court in all cases — this causes undue hardship to families. PROCESS FOR DEATH CLAIM WITHOUT NOMINATION: 1. Widow/widower can claim using legal heir certificate from SDM/Tehsildar 2. Children can claim with their birth certificates + death certificate of member 3. EPFO processes under Para 70(ii) of EPF Scheme — equal shares among family members 4. EPS family pension: Automatically payable to spouse. Submit Form 10D. 5. EDLI benefit: Form 5-IF. Same documents. DOCUMENTS NEEDED FOR DEATH CLAIM: - Death certificate of member - Legal heir certificate (SDM/Tehsildar) OR succession certificate from court if no legal heir cert available - Bank account details of claimant - Aadhaar of claimant (for UAN-linked accounts) - Age proof of widow/children - Form 20 (EPF), Form 10D (EPS pension), Form 5-IF (EDLI)

TDS applicable if service < 5 years AND withdrawal exceeds Rs.50,000. Resident rate with valid PAN: 10%. Resident without valid PAN: 20%. Non-resident: 30% plus 4% cess. No TDS if service >= 5 years or transfer of funds.

        - Retirement
        - Permanent migration abroad
        - Death
        
        INTEREST ON INOPERATIVE ACCOUNTS:
        - At present, all accounts earn interest up to age 58 of the member
        - After account becomes inoperative, no further interest is credited
        - Account becomes inoperative after 36 months from date of retirement (55 years+) or permanent migration
        
        WHAT TO DO WITH INOPERATIVE ACCOUNT:
        - If still employed in covered establishment: Get amount transferred to new account (online or offline Form 13)
        - If retired: Withdraw the amount by submitting withdrawal claim
        
        UNCLAIMED PF (SENIOR CITIZENS FUND):
        Unclaimed EPF amounts are transferred to Senior Citizens Welfare Fund (SCWF) after 7 years of becoming inoperative. However, members can still claim this amount from EPFO.

        - Trace the account using establishment name or PF number
        - Contact the employer for details
        - Submit withdrawal claim to concerned Regional PF Office with proper KYC documentation

An EPF account is classified as Inoperative when no contribution has been received for 3 years after:
        - Retirement
        - Permanent migration abroad
        - Death
        
        INTEREST ON INOPERATIVE ACCOUNTS:
        - At present, all accounts earn interest up to age 58 of the member
        - After account becomes inoperative, no further interest is credited
        - Account becomes inoperative after 36 months from date of retirement (55 years+) or permanent migration
        
        WHAT TO DO WITH INOPERATIVE ACCOUNT:
        - If still employed in covered establishment: Get amount transferred to new account (online or offline Form 13)
        - If retired: Withdraw the amount by submitting withdrawal claim
        
        UNCLAIMED PF (SENIOR CITIZENS FUND):
        Unclaimed EPF amounts are transferred to Senior Citizens Welfare Fund (SCWF) after 7 years of becoming inoperative. However, members can still claim this amount from EPFO.
        
        HOW TO CLAIM INOPERATIVE ACCOUNT:
        - Trace the account using establishment name or PF number
        - Contact the employer for details
        - Submit withdrawal claim to concerned Regional PF Office with proper KYC documentation
        
        PASSBOOK FOR INOPERATIVE ACCOUNTS:
        - For exempted establishments: Passbook not visible on UAN portal; contact establishment directly
        - For non-exempted: Login to UAN portal passbook section

GSR 604(E) dated 31.08.2021, effective 01.04.2022 — Amendment to Section 192A of Income Tax Act read with Finance Act 2020 changes. CURRENT TDS RULES (effective April 2022): WHEN TDS APPLIES: Service < 5 years AND withdrawal amount > Rs.50,000. WHEN TDS DOES NOT APPLY: - Service >= 5 years (combined across employers if PF transferred) - Withdrawal amount <= Rs.50,000 - Transfer of PF (not withdrawal) - Partial advance (Form 31) - Form 15G / 15H submitted with valid PAN - Service terminated due to ill health or employer winding up (beyond employee control) TDS RATES: Resident with valid PAN: 10% (Section 194A rate) Resident without PAN or invalid PAN: 20% (Section 206AA — higher of 20% or applicable rate) Non-resident with valid PAN: 30% or DTAA rate (whichever beneficial); + 4% Health & Education Cess Non-resident without PAN: 30% + cess; no threshold exemption FORM 15G / 15H: Form 15G: For residents below 60 years. Total estimated income < Rs.2,50,000 for the FY. Must have PAN. Form 15H: For senior citizens 60+. Total estimated income < Rs.3,00,000 for the FY. Must have PAN. Must be submitted BEFORE the withdrawal claim. Requires 2 copies. Cannot retroactively avoid TDS. EMPLOYER'S OBLIGATION: Employer (EPFO) must deduct TDS, deposit by 7th of following month (30th April for March). Issue Form 16A (TDS certificate) within 15 days of due date of quarterly TDS return. Report in Form 26Q (residents) or Form 27Q (non-residents). MEMBER'S RECOURSE IF TDS DEDUCTED: Claim TDS refun

WHEN TDS DOES NOT APPLY: - Service >= 5 years (combined across employers if PF transferred) - Withdrawal amount <= Rs.50,000 - Transfer of PF (not withdrawal) - Partial advance (Form 31) - Form 15G / 15H submitted with valid PAN - Service terminated due to ill health or employer winding up (beyond employee control) TDS RATES: Resident with valid PAN: 10% (Section 194A rate) Resident without PAN or invalid PAN: 20% (Section 206AA — higher of 20% or applicable rate) Non-resident with valid PAN: 30% or DTAA rate (whichever beneficial); + 4% Health & Education Cess Non-resident without PAN: 30% + cess; no threshold exemption FORM 15G / 15H: Form 15G: For residents below 60 years. [Source 2] doc_id=TDS_PRACTICAL_GUIDE_2024_chunk_1 | date=2024-04-01 | domain=pf
(basic exemption for senior citizens). 2024 UPDATES — KEY CHANGES: 1. TDS on Transfer NOT applicable: Transfer of PF from one account to another is not treated as withdrawal — no TDS even if service < 5 years. 2. Advances/Partial withdrawals (Form 31): TDS is NOT applicable on partial withdrawal advances. 3. Split accounts (EPF + VPF): TDS calculated on total taxable withdrawal, not split separately. 4. Non-resident (NRI) members: TDS at 30% (or DTAA rate if lower) — Form 15G/15H not available to NRIs. File Form 10F for DTAA benefit. WHAT HAPPENS AFTER TDS DEDUCTION: - Form 16A (TDS certificate) is issued by EPFO within 15 days from due date of TDS return - TDS can be claimed as credit in your Income Tax Return (ITR) - If your total income is below taxable limit, you can claim TDS refund from Income Tax Department EXAMPLE SCENARIOS: Scenario A: Service 4 years, balance Rs.80,000, valid PAN → TDS = 10% of Rs.80,000 = Rs.8,000. Submit Form 15G to avoid if income < Rs.2.5 lakh. Scenario B: Service 4 years, balance Rs.40,000, valid PAN → NO TDS (below Rs.50,000 threshold). Scenario C: Service 6 years, balance Rs.2,00,000 → NO TDS (service >= 5 years). Scenario D: Service 3 years, no PAN → TDS = 20% on amount above threshold. TAX YEAR NOTE: For withdrawal spanning two financial years, TDS is calculated in the year of withdrawal. Always provide latest Form 15G for the current financial year (April to March).

---

Form 10D: Monthly Pension claim. For members/family seeking EPS pension.
Form 31: Non-refundable Advance / Partial withdrawal. Online if KYC complete.
Form 20: Death case — PF withdrawal by nominee/legal heir.
Form 5-IF: EDLI (Insurance) benefit claim by nominee/legal heir.
Composite Claim Form (Aadhaar): Replaces Forms 19, 10C, 31 — can be submitted without employer attestation. Online and offline.
Composite Claim Form (Non-Aadhaar): Replaces Forms 19, 10C, 31 — requires employer attestation. Offline only.
Another Composite Form: Replaces Forms 20, 10D, 5-IF for death/pension cases.

TRANSFER FORMS:
Form 13 / Form 13(R): PF transfer request from previous to current account. Online preferred; physical only for exempted-to-exempted.
Annexure-K: Transfer from exempted trust — details sent by trust to EPFO/receiving trust.

MEMBER REGISTRATION / KYC:
Form 11 (New): Declaration of previous EPF service on new joining. Enables auto-transfer.
Form 2: Nomination form for EPF, EPS, and EDLI. Family

Practical guide on TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) for PF withdrawals, reflecting rules effective from April 2022 and updated in 2024. THE SIMPLE RULE — TWO CONDITIONS MUST BOTH BE TRUE FOR TDS: Condition 1: Total service is LESS than 5 years (counting service across all employers if PF was transferred) AND Condition 2: Withdrawal amount is MORE than Rs.50,000 If EITHER condition is false → NO TDS is deducted. COUNTING THE 5 YEARS: - Service includes all employers where PF was contributed AND transferred to your current account - Example: 3 years at Company A (transferred) + 2.5 years at Company B = 5.5 years total → NO TDS - If you did NOT transfer PF from previous employer, each account is treated separately - TDS applies per withdrawal claim, not per employer TDS RATES (for residential Indians with PAN): Scenario | TDS Rate PAN valid + service < 5 yrs + > 50k | 10% No valid PAN + service < 5 yrs + >50k | 20% Service 5+ years (regardless of amount)| 0% Amount ≤ Rs.50,000 | 0% HOW TO SUBMIT FORM 15G (to get zero TDS): WHO: Resident individual below 60 years whose estimated total income for the year is below Rs.2,50,000 (i.e., below basic exemption limit). WHEN: Submit BEFORE filing the withdrawal claim. Cannot be submitted after TDS is deducted. WHERE (Online): Login to member portal > Online Services > Claim > You'll see a checkbox/link to upload Form 15G before submitting the claim. Upload 2 copies. WHERE (Offline): Submit 2 physical copies along with claim form to PF office.

Standard 12% rate applies. Para 68-L remains in the Scheme for future government-declared epidemics but the COVID-specific variant is no longer active. Members who availed COVID advance cannot avail again for the same account. HISTORICAL IMPORTANCE: If your passbook shows a claim from May-July 2020 for 75% advance, that was the COVID advance. If you withdrew it, your current balance is lower and you cannot claim that advance again.

[Source 2] doc_id=INOPERATIVE_ACCOUNTS | date=2024-01-01 | domain=pf
An EPF account is classified as Inoperative when no contribution has been received for 3 years after:
        - Retirement
        - Permanent migration abroad
        - Death
        
        INTEREST ON INOPERATIVE ACCOUNTS:
        - At present, all accounts earn interest up to age 58 of the member
        - After account becomes inoperative, no further interest is credited

        - Contact the employer for details

WHEN: Submit BEFORE filing the withdrawal claim. Cannot be submitted after TDS is deducted. WHERE (Online): Lo

Step 2: Verify KYC is complete. → Go to Manage > KYC. Confirm bank account shows 'Digitally Approved by Empl

NOTE: For complex claims (higher pension, death claims, closed establishment issues), the full web portal at unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in is still recommended.

        - Retirement

        INTEREST ON INOPERATIVE ACCOUNTS:
        - At present, all accounts earn interest up to age 58 of the member
        - After account becomes inoperative, no further interest is credited
        - Account becomes inoperative after 36 months from date of retirement (55 years+) or permanent migration
        
        WHAT TO DO WITH INOPERATIVE ACCOUNT:
        - If still employed in covered establishment: Get amount transferred to new account (online or offline Form 13)
        - If retired: Withdraw the amount by submitting withdrawal claim
        
        UNCLAIMED PF (SENIOR CITIZENS FUND):
        Unclaimed EPF amounts are transferred to Senior Citizens Welfare Fund (SCWF) after 7 years of becoming inoperative. However, members can still claim this amount from EPFO.

        - Trace the account using establishment name or PF number
        - Contact the employer for details
        - Submit withdrawal claim to concerned Regional PF Office with proper KYC documentation
        
        PASSBOOK FOR INOPERATIVE ACCOUNTS:

Submit Form 15G to avoid if income < Rs.2.5 lakh. Scenario B: Service 4 years, balance Rs.40,000, valid PAN → NO TDS (below Rs.50,000 threshold). Scenario C: Service 6 years, balance Rs.2,00,000 → NO TDS (service >= 5 years). Scenario D: Service 3 years, no PAN → TDS = 20% on amount above threshold. TAX YEAR NOTE: For withdrawal spanning two financial years, TDS is calculated in the year of withdrawal. Always provide latest Form 15G for the current financial year (April to March).

Form 31: Non-refundable Advance / Partial withdrawal. Online if KYC complete.
Form 20: Death case — PF withdrawal by nominee/legal heir.
Form 5-IF: EDLI (Insurance) benefit claim by nominee/legal heir.
Composite Claim Form (Aadhaar): Replaces Forms 19, 10C, 31 — can be submitted without employer attestation. Online and offline.
Composite Claim Form (Non-Aadhaar): Replaces Forms 19, 10C, 31 — requires employer attestation. Offline only.
Another Composite Form: Replaces Forms 20, 10D, 5-IF for death/pension cases.

TRANSFER FORMS:
Form 13 / Form 13(R): PF transfer request from previous to current account. Online preferred; physical only for exempted-to-exempted.
Annexure-K: Transfer from exempted trust — details sent by trust to EPFO/receiving trust.

MEMBER REGISTRATION / KYC:
Form 11 (New): Declaration of previous EPF service on new joining. Enables auto-transfer.
Form 2: Nomination form for EPF, EPS, and EDLI. Family and nominee details.
Form 3A/ECR Copy: Contribution card. Member can demand from employer.

TAX FORMS:
Form 15G: Declaration for zero TDS if total income below Rs.2.5 lakh (non-senior citizens, any age). Needs PAN

UMANG (Unified Mobile Application for New-age Governance) app provides access to all major EPFO services on mobile without needing to login to the full web portal. HOW TO ACCESS: Download UMANG from Google Play Store / Apple App Store. Search 'EPFO' after login. SERVICES AVAILABLE ON UMANG: 1. View EPF Passbook (balance check) 2. Raise claim — Form 19 (final settlement), Form 10C (pension/scheme cert), Form 31 (advance) 3. Track claim status 4. KYC update (Aadhaar, PAN, bank account) 5. UAN activation 6. e-Nomination filing 7. Mark date of exit (after 60 days of leaving) 8. Transfer claim (Form 13) initiation 9. Raise EPFiGMS grievance 10. View pension passbook (for pensioners) 11. Generate UAN (if not already generated) 12. Download UAN card 13. Link Aadhaar via OTP (self KYC without employer) UMANG LOGIN: Use your UAN and EPFO member portal password. Same credentials. ADVANTAGE OVER WEB PORTAL: Works on low-end smartphones. Faster for simple tasks like balance check. OTP-based Aadhaar linking works offline-equivalent. UMANG TOLL-FREE: 1800-11-5246. NOTE: For complex claims (higher pension, death claims, closed establishment issues), the full web portal at unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in is still recommended.

Practical guide on TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) for PF withdrawals, reflecting rules effective from April 2022 and updated in 2024. THE SIMPLE RULE — TWO CONDITIONS MUST BOTH BE TRUE FOR TDS: Condition 1: Total service is LESS than 5 years (counting service across all employers if PF was transferred) AND Condition 2: Withdrawal amount is MORE than Rs.50,000 If EITHER condition is false → NO TDS is deducted. COUNTING THE 5 YEARS: - Service includes all employers where PF was contributed AND transferred to your current account - Example: 3 years at Company A (transferred) + 2.5 years at Company B = 5.5 years total → NO TDS - If you did NOT transfer PF from previous employer, each account is treated separately - TDS applies per withdrawal claim, not per employer TDS RATES (for residential Indians with PAN): Scenario | TDS Rate PAN valid + service < 5 yrs + > 50k | 10% No valid PAN + service < 5 yrs + >50k | 20% Service 5+ years (regardless of amount)| 0% Amount ≤ Rs.50,000 | 0% HOW TO SUBMIT FORM 15G (to get zero TDS): WHO: Resident individual below 60 years whose estimated total income for the year is below Rs.2,50,000 (i.e., below basic exemption limit). WHEN: Submit BEFORE filing the withdrawal claim. Cannot be submitted after TDS is deducted. WHERE (Online): Log

USER QUERY:
My KYC is partial on EPFO portal. What to do?

DOMAIN: pf

RETRIEVED PASSAGES:
[Source 1] doc_id=FAQ_CLAIM_108 | date=2024-01-01 | domain=pf
Q: Who allots the Universal Account Number? A: The UAN is allotted by EPFO on the request of the Employer and populated in the Employer's login at the unified portal www.unifiedportal-emp.epfindia.gov.in. The UAN can also be generated by any individual using their Aadhaar if UAN is not already generated.

---

[Source 2] doc_id=KYC_REJECTION_REASONS_RESOLUTION | date=2024-01-01 | domain=pf
KYC (Know Your Customer) documents can be rejected by EPFO or employer for several reasons. Understanding the exact rejection reason is the first step to resolution. HOW TO CHECK KYC STATUS AND REJECTION REASON: Login to https://unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in/memberinterface/ > Manage > KYC. Status will show: Pending / Digitally Approved by Employer / Rejected / Verified by UIDAI. Click on the rejected entry to see the reason. COMMON KYC REJECTION REASONS AND RESOLUTION: 1. NAME MISMATCH (Aadhaar vs PF Records): Reason: Name on Aadhaar does not match name registered in EPFO records. Resolution: Submit Joint Declaration (JD) form online to correct name in PF records first, then re-link Aadhaar. OR update Aadhaar to match PF records via UIDAI. Documents needed: marriage certificate if name changed, school certificate or passport showing original name. 2. DATE OF BIRTH MISMATCH (Aadhaar vs PF Records): Reason: Date of birth on Aadhaar differs from DOB in EPFO system. Resolution: Submit Joint Declaration for DOB correction with supporting document (birth certificate, school certificate, passport, or Aadhaar itself if within ±3 years of PF record). Employer must approve JD. 3. AADHAAR NOT LINKED TO MOBILE NUMBER: Reason: OTP cannot be sent during KYC verification as Aadhaar has no mobile registered. Resolution: Visit nearest Aadhaar Seva Kendra (ASK) to register/update mobile number in Aadhaar. Or visit UIDAI website at https://uidai.gov.in. After updating, retry KYC after 48-7

---

[Source 3] doc_id=FAQ_KYC | date=2023-01-01 | domain=pf

}

Complete reference for EPFO forms and their uses.

WITHDRAWAL / SETTLEMENT FORMS:
Form 19: PF Final Settlement (Full withdrawal). Online if KYC complete.
Form 10C: Scheme Certificate (retain pension membership) OR Withdrawal Benefit (if service < 10 years). Online if KYC complete.
Form 10D: Monthly Pension claim. For members/family seeking EPS pension.
Form 31: Non-refundable Advance / Partial withdrawal. Online if KYC complete.
Form 20: Death case — PF withdrawal by nominee/legal heir.
Form 5-IF: EDLI (Insurance) benefit claim by nominee/legal heir.
Composite Claim Form (Aadhaar): Replaces Forms 19, 10C, 31 — can be submitted without employer attestation. Online and offline.
Composite Claim Form (Non-Aadhaar): Replaces Forms 19, 10C, 31 — requires employer attestation. Offline only.
Another Composite Form: Replaces Forms 20, 10D, 5-IF

Another Composite Form: Replaces Forms 20, 10D, 5-IF for death/pension cases.

TRANSFER FORMS:
Form 13 / Form 13(R): PF transfer request from previous to current account. Online preferred; physical only for exempted-to-exempted.
Annexure-K: Transfer from exempted trust — details sent by trust to EPFO/receiving trust.

Form 11 (New): Declaration of previous EPF service on new joining. Enables auto-transfer.
Form 2: Nomination form for EPF, EPS, and EDLI. Family and nominee details.
Form 3A/ECR Copy: Contribution card. Member can demand from employer.

TAX FORMS:
Form 15G: Declaration for zero TDS if total income below Rs.2.5 lakh (non-senior citizens, any age). Needs PAN

THE SIMPLE RULE — TWO CONDITIONS MUST BOTH BE TRUE FOR TDS: Condition 1: Total service is LESS than 5 years (counting service across all employers if PF was transferred) AND Condition 2: Withdrawal amount is MORE than Rs.50,000 If EITHER condition is false → NO TDS is deducted. COUNTING THE 5 YEARS: - Service includes all employers where PF was contributed AND transferred to your current account - Example: 3 years at Company A (transferred) + 2.5 years at Company B = 5.5 years total → NO TDS - If you did NOT transfer PF from previous employer, each account is treated separately - TDS applies per withdrawal claim, not per employer TDS RATES (for residential Indians with PAN): Scenario | TDS Rate PAN valid + service < 5 yrs + > 50k | 10% No valid PAN + service < 5 yrs + >50k | 20% Service 5+ years (regardless of amount)| 0% Amount ≤ Rs.50,000 | 0% HOW TO SUBMIT FORM 15G (to get zero TDS): WHO: Resident individual below 60 years whose estimated total income for the year is below Rs.2,50,000 (i.e., below basic exemption limit). WHEN: Submit BEFORE filing the withdrawal claim. Cannot be submitted after TDS is deducted. WHERE (Online): Login to member portal > Online Services > Claim > You'll see a checkbox/link to upload Form 15G before submitting the claim. Upload 2 copies. WHERE (Offline): Submit 2 physical copies along with claim form to PF office.

        - Login at https://unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in > View > Passbook > Enter UAN, password, captcha
        - Direct access: https://passbook.epfindia.gov.in/MemberPassBook/Login
        - Download: Go to Download menu > Download Passbook > PDF download link available
        - Via UMANG App > EPFO > Employee Centric Services > Passbook
        - Via missed call to 011-22901406
        - Via SMS to 7738299899 in 10 languages
        
        PASSBOOK NOT AVAILABLE:
        If working in an Exempted establishment, passbook is not available on UAN portal. Contact establishment directly for PF statement.
        
        INTEREST CREDITING:
        - Interest is credited on monthly running balance basis
        - Credited annually after Central Government declares rate
        - Delay in passbook update does not cause financial loss — interest is added to closing balance and becomes opening balance for next year
        - If member withdraws before interest is updated, due interest is auto-calculated and paid at claim settlement
        
        SERVICE HISTORY:
        Login to e-Passbook > Click on Service History to see employment details across multiple establishments.

[Source 1] doc_id=NEWS_EPF_CLAIM_REJECTION_REASONS_2024 | date=2024-01-01 | domain=pf
Common EPF claim rejection reasons and resolutions compiled from EPFO field office reports and member portal data. TOP 10 CLAIM REJECTION REASONS: 1. BANK ACCOUNT NOT VERIFIED / IFSC INCORRECT Fix: Re-enter bank details in KYC section. Get current IFSC from bank. Wait for employer approval. 2. DATE OF EXIT NOT UPDATED Fix: If 60+ days since leaving — self-update via Manage > Mark Exit. If less than 60 days — ask employer to update in their portal. 3. NAME MISMATCH (Aadhaar vs PF records) Fix: Submit Joint Declaration for name correction. Employer approves. Then re-link Aadhaar. 4. MEMBER ALREADY SETTLED (Duplicate claim attempt) Fix: Check passbook — if first claim was settled, the amount should be in your bank. If not, file grievance. 5. UAN NOT AADHAAR SEEDED Fix: Link Aadhaar via member portal > Manage > KYC > Aadhaar. Wait for UIDAI verification. 6. CLAIM FOR WRONG PURPOSE (e.g. Form 19 filed while still employed) Fix: Wait 2 months after leaving service, then file again. 7. INSUFFICIENT SERVICE FOR ADVANCE TYPE Fix: Check eligibility table. Select correct advance purpose matching your service period. 8. DOJ (Date of Joining) NOT UPDATED OR INCORRECT Fix: Contact employer to update DOJ via employer portal. Raise JD request if employer is unavailable.

prompt:
USER QUERY:
My KYC is incomplete on EPFO portal. What to do?

DOMAIN: pf

RETRIEVED PASSAGES:
[Source 1] doc_id=KYC_REJECTION_REASONS_RESOLUTION | date=2024-01-01 | domain=pf
KYC (Know Your Customer) documents can be rejected by EPFO or employer for several reasons. Understanding the exact rejection reason is the first step to resolution. HOW TO CHECK KYC STATUS AND REJECTION REASON: Login to https://unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in/memberinterface/ > Manage > KYC. Status will show: Pending / Digitally Approved by Employer / Rejected / Verified by UIDAI. Click on the rejected entry to see the reason. COMMON KYC REJECTION REASONS AND RESOLUTION: 1. NAME MISMATCH (Aadhaar vs PF Records): Reason: Name on Aadhaar does not match name registered in EPFO records. Resolution: Submit Joint Declaration (JD) form online to correct name in PF records first, then re-link Aadhaar. OR update Aadhaar to match PF records via UIDAI. Documents needed: marriage certificate if name changed, school certificate or passport showing original name. 2. DATE OF BIRTH MISMATCH (Aadhaar vs PF Records): Reason: Date of birth on Aadhaar differs from DOB in EPFO system. full_prompt:
USER QUERY:
My KYC is incomplete on EPFO portal. What to do?

DOMAIN: pf

RETRIEVED PASSAGES:
[Source 1] doc_id=KYC_REJECTION_REASONS_RESOLUTION | date=2024-01-01 | domain=pf
KYC (Know Your Customer) documents can be rejected by EPFO or employer for several reasons. Understanding the exact rejection reason is the first step to resolution. HOW TO CHECK KYC STATUS AND REJECTION REASON: Login to https://unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in/memberinterface/ > Manage > KYC. Status will show: Pending / Digitally Approved by Employer / Rejected / Verified by UIDAI. Click on the rejected entry to see the reason. COMMON KYC REJECTION REASONS AND RESOLUTION: 1. NAME MISMATCH (Aadhaar vs PF Records): Reason: Name on Aadhaar does not match name registered in EPFO records. Resolution: Submit Joint Declaration (JD) form online to correct name in PF records first, then re-link Aadhaar. OR update Aadhaar to match PF records via UIDAI. Documents needed: marriage certificate if name changed, school certificate or passport showing original name. 2. DATE OF BIRTH MISMATCH (Aadhaar vs PF Records): Reason: Date of birth on Aadhaar differs from DOB in EPFO system. Resolution: Submit Joint Declaration for DOB correction with supporting document (birth certificate, school certificate, passport, or Aadhaar itself if within ±3 years of PF record). Employer must approve JD. 3. AADHAAR NOT LINKED TO MOBILE NUMBER: Reason: OTP cannot be sent during KYC verification as Aadhaar has no mobile registered. Resolution: Visit nearest Aadhaar Seva Kendra (ASK) to register/update mobile number in Aadhaar. Or visit UIDAI website at https://uidai.gov.in. After updating, retry KYC after 48-7

---

[Source 2] doc_id=FAQ_CLAIM_130 | date=2024-01-01 | domain=pf
Q: How can I update KYC? A: Member can update KYC details online in EPFO's UAN Portal at https://unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in



SLOTS FILLED:
{
  "intent": "kyc_issue",
  "kyc_status": "incomplete"
}

Produce the final answer now. IMPORTANT: every factual claim must include [DOC_ID] in brackets — no exceptions. If you cannot cite, omit the claim.

**Process [KYC_REJECTION_REASONS_RESOLUTION]:**
1. Login to unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in using UAN.
2. Manage → KYC.
3. Update the mismatched document (Aadhaar/PAN/bank).
4. Wait for employer digital approval.

**For Aadhaar name mismatch:** Correct via UIDAI portal first, then re-seed.

DOMAIN: pf

RETRIEVED PASSAGES:
[Source 1] doc_id=CIRC_ADVANCE_MEDICAL | date=2020-03-28 | domain=pf
COVID-19 / EPIDEMIC (Para 68-L): Non-refundable advance available once during declared epidemic. Amount: 75% of PF balance or 3 months' wages/DA, whichever is less.

---

[Source 2] doc_id=GAZETTE_TDS_AMDT_2022 | date=2022-04-01 | domain=pf
GSR 604(E) dated 31.08.2021, effective 01.04.2022 — Amendment to Section 192A of Income Tax Act read with Finance Act 2020 changes. CURRENT TDS RULES (effective April 2022): WHEN TDS APPLIES: Service < 5 years AND withdrawal amount > Rs.50,000. WHEN TDS DOES NOT APPLY: - Service >= 5 years (combined across employers if PF transferred) - Withdrawal amount <= Rs.50,000 - Transfer of PF (not withdrawal) - Partial advance (Form 31) - Form 15G / 15H submitted with valid PAN - Service terminated due to ill health or employer winding up (beyond employee control) TDS RATES: Resident with valid PAN: 10% (Section 194A rate) Resident without PAN or invalid PAN: 20% (Section 206AA — higher of 20% or applicable rate) Non-resident with valid PAN: 30% or DTAA rate (whichever beneficial); + 4% Health & Education Cess Non-resident without PAN: 30% + cess; no threshold exemption FORM 15G / 15H: Form 15G: For residents below 60 years. Total estimated income < Rs.2,50,000 for the FY. Must have PAN. Form 15H: For senior citizens 60+. Total estimated income < Rs.3,00,000 for the FY. Must have PAN. Must be submitted BEFORE the withdrawal claim. Requires 2 copies. Cannot retroactively avoid TDS. EMPLOYER'S OBLIGATION: Employer (EPFO) must deduct TDS, deposit by 7th of following month (30th April for March). Issue Form 16A (TDS certificate) within 15 days of due date of quarterly TDS return. Report in Form 26Q (residents) or Form 27Q (non-residents). MEMBER'S RECOURSE IF TDS DEDUCTED: Claim TDS refun

---

An EPF account is classified as Inoperative when no contribution has been received for 3 years after:
        - Retirement
        - Permanent migration abroad
        - Death
        
        INTEREST ON INOPERATIVE ACCOUNTS:
        - At present, all accounts earn interest up to age 58 of the member
        - After account becomes inoperative, no further interest is credited
        - Account becomes inoperative after 36 months from date of retirement (55 years+) or permanent migration
        
        WHAT TO DO WITH INOPERATIVE ACCOUNT:
        - If still employed in covered establishment: Get amount transferred to new account (online or offline Form 13)
        - If retired: Withdraw the amount by submitting withdrawal claim
        
        UNCLAIMED PF (SENIOR CITIZENS FUND):
        Unclaimed EPF amounts are transferred to Senior Citizens Welfare Fund (SCWF) after 7 years of becoming inoperative. However, members can still claim this amount from EPFO.
        
        HOW TO CLAIM INOPERATIVE ACCOUNT:
        - Trace the account using establishment name or PF number
        - Contact the employer for details
        - Submit withdrawal claim to concerned Regional PF Office with proper KYC documentation
        
        PASSBOOK FOR INOPERATIVE ACCOUNTS:

---

[Source 2] doc_id=FORMS_REFERENCE_COMPLETE | date=2024-01-01 | domain=pf
Complete reference for EPFO forms and their uses.

WITHDRAWAL / SETTLEMENT FORMS:
Form 19: PF Final Settlement (Full withdrawal). Online if KYC complete.
Form 10C: Scheme Certificate (retain pension membership) OR Withdrawal Benefit (if service < 10 years). Online if KYC complete.
Form 10D: Monthly Pension claim. For members/family seeking EPS pension.
Form 31: Non-refundable Advance / Partial withdrawal. Online if KYC complete.
Form 20: Death case — PF withdrawal by nominee/legal heir.
Form 5-IF: EDLI (Insurance) benefit claim by nominee/legal heir.
Composite Claim Form (Aadhaar): Replaces Forms 19, 10C, 31 — can be submitted without employer attestation. Online and offline.
Composite Claim Form (Non-Aadhaar): Replaces Forms 19, 10C, 31 — requires employer attestation. Offline only.
Another Composite Form: Replaces Forms 20, 10D, 5-IF for death/pension cases.

TRANSFER FORMS:
Form 13 / Form 13(R): PF transfer request from previous to current account. Online preferred; physical only for exempted-to-exempted.
Annexure-K: Transfer from exempted trust — details sent by trust to EPFO/r

WITHDRAWAL / SETTLEMENT FORMS:
Form 19: PF Final Settlement (Full withdrawal). Online if KYC complete.
Form 10C: Scheme Certificate (retain pension membership) OR Withdrawal Benefit (if service < 10 years). Online if KYC complete.
Form 10D: Monthly Pension claim. For members/family seeking EPS pension.
Form 31: Non-refundable Advance / Partial withdrawal. Online if KYC complete.
Form 20: Death case — PF withdrawal by nominee/legal heir.
Form 5-IF: EDLI (Insurance) benefit claim by nominee/legal heir.
Composite Claim Form (Aadhaar): Replaces Forms 19, 10C, 31 — can be submitted without employer attestation. Online and offline.
Composite Claim Form (Non-Aadhaar): Replaces Forms 19, 10C, 31 — requires employer attestation. Offline only.
Another Composite Form: Replaces Forms 20, 10D, 5-IF for death/pension cases.

TRANSFER FORMS:
Form 13 / Form 13(R): PF transfer request from previous to current account. Online preferred; physical only for exempted-to-exempted.
Annexure-K: Transfer from exempted trust — details sent by trust to EPFO/receiving trust.

MEMBER REGISTRATION / KYC:
Form 11 (New): Declaration of previous EPF service on new joining. Enables auto-transfer.
Form 2: Nomination form for EPF, EPS, and EDLI. Family and nominee details.
Form 3A/ECR Copy: Contribution card. Member can demand from employer.

TAX FORMS:
Form 15G: Declaration for zero TDS if total income below Rs.2.5 lakh (non-senior citizens, any age). Needs PAN

EPF Passbook shows monthly contributions, transfer credits, and interest credited.
        
        HOW TO ACCESS PASSBOOK:
        - Login at https://unifiedportal-mem.epfindia.gov.in > View > Passbook > Enter UAN, password, captcha
        - Direct access: https://passbook.epfindia.gov.in/MemberPassBook/Login
        - Download: Go to Download menu > Download Passbook > PDF download link available
        - Via UMANG App > EPFO > Employee Centric Services > Passbook
        - Via missed call to 011-22901406
        - Via SMS to 7738299899 in 10 languages
        
        PASSBOOK NOT AVAILABLE:
        If working in an Exempted establishment, passbook is not available on UAN portal. Contact establishment directly for PF statement.
        
        INTEREST CREDITING:
        - Interest is credited on monthly running balance basis
        - Credited annually after Central Government declares rate
        - Delay in passbook update does not cause financial loss — interest is added to closing balance and becomes opening balance for next year
        - If member withdraws before interest is updated, due interest is auto-calculated and paid at claim settlement
        
        SERVICE HISTORY:
        Login to e-Passbook > Click on Service History to see employment details across multiple establishments.

prompt:
USER QUERY:
Resigned 2 months ago after 10 years service. UAN active, KYC complete. Can I withdraw full PF?

DOMAIN: pf

RETRIEVED PASSAGES:
[Source 1] doc_id=CASE_BHAVNA_BHATT_DELHI_HC_2018 | date=2018-01-01 | domain=pf
Case: Bhavna Bhatt v. Regional Provident Fund Commissioner (Delhi High Court, 2018). Relevance: Governs death claim process when no valid nomination exists. FACTS: Member died without having filed Form 2 nomination. Widow approached EPFO for PF settlement. EPFO demanded succession certificate from civil court, causing delay. HELD: Legal heir certificate from competent authority (Tehsildar / Revenue Officer / SDM) or court affidavit is sufficient for PF claim settlement when no nomination exists. EPFO cannot insist on succession certificate from civil court in all cases — this causes undue hardship to families. PROCESS FOR DEATH CLAIM WITHOUT NOMINATION: 1. Widow/widower can claim using legal heir certificate from SDM/Tehsildar 2. Children can claim with their birth certificates + death certificate of member 3. EPFO processes under Para 70(ii) of EPF Scheme — equal shares among family members 4. EPS family pension: Automatically payable to spouse. Submit Form 10D. 5. EDLI benefit: Form 5-IF. Same documents. DOCUMENTS NEEDED FOR DEATH CLAIM: - Death certificate of member - Legal heir certificate (SDM/Tehsildar) OR succession certificate from court if no legal heir cert available - Bank account details of claimant - Aadhaar of claimant (for UAN-linked accounts)

Advances/Partial withdrawals (Form 31): TDS is NOT applicable on partial withdrawal advances. 3. Split accounts (EPF + VPF): TDS calculated on total taxable withdrawal, not split separately. 4. TAX YEAR NOTE: For withdrawal spanning two financial years, TDS is calculated in the year of withdrawal. Always provide latest Form 15G for the current financial year (April to March).

[Source 2] doc_id=TDS_PRACTICAL_GUIDE_2024 | date=2024-04-01 | domain=pf
Practical guide on TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) for PF withdrawals, reflecting rules effective from April 2022 and updated in 2024. THE SIMPLE RULE — TWO CONDITIONS MUST BOTH BE TRUE FOR TDS: Condition 1: Total service is LESS than 5 years (counting service across all employers if PF was transferred) AND Condition 2: Withdrawal amount is MORE than Rs.50,000 If EITHER condition is false → NO TDS is deducted. COUNTING THE 5 YEARS: - Service includes all employers where PF was contributed AND transferred to your current account - Example: 3 years at Company A (transferred) + 2.5 years at Company B = 5.5 years total → NO TDS - If you did NOT transfer PF from previous employer, each account is treated separately - TDS applies per withdrawal claim, not per employer TDS RATES (for residential Indians with PAN): Scenario | TDS Rate PAN valid + service < 5 yrs + > 50k | 10% No valid PAN + service < 5 yrs + >50k | 20% Service 5+ years (regardless of amount)| 0% Amount ≤ Rs.50,000 | 0% HOW TO SUBMIT FORM 15G (to get zero TDS): WHO: Resident individual below 60 years whose estimated total income for the year is below Rs.2,50,000 (i.e., below basic exemption limit). WHEN: Submit BEFORE filing the withdrawal claim. Cannot be submitted after TDS is deducted. WHERE (Online): Login to member portal > Online Services > Claim > You'll see a checkbox/link to upload Form 15G before submitting the claim. Upload 2 copies. WHERE (Offline): Submit 2 physical copies along with claim form to PF office.

---

[Source 3] doc_id=CASE_BHAVNA_BHATT_DELHI_HC_2018 | date=2018-01-01 | domain=pf
Case: Bhavna Bhatt v. Regional Provident Fund Commissioner (Delhi High Court, 2018). Relevance: Governs death claim process when no valid nomination exists. FACTS: Member died without having filed Form 2 nomination. Widow approached EPFO for PF settlement. EPFO demanded succession certificate from civil court, causing delay. HELD: Legal heir certificate from competent authority (Tehsildar / Revenue Officer / SDM) or court affidavit is sufficient for PF claim settlement when no nomination exists. EPFO cannot insist on succession certificate from civil court in all cases — this causes undue hardship to families. PROCESS FOR DEATH CLAIM WITHOUT NOMINATION: 1. Widow/widower can claim using legal heir certificate from SDM/Tehsildar 2. Children can claim with their birth certificates + death certificate of member 3. EPFO processes under Para 70(ii) of EPF Scheme — equal shares among family members 4. EPS family pension: Automatically payable to spouse. Submit Form 10D. 5. EDLI benefit: Form 5-IF. Same documents. DOCUMENTS NEEDED FOR DEATH CLAIM: - Death certificate of member - Legal heir certificate (SDM/Tehsildar) OR succession certificate from court if no legal heir cert available - Bank account details of claimant - Aadhaar of claimant (for UAN-linked accounts) - Age proof of widow/children - Form 20 (EPF), Form 10D (EPS pension), Form 5-IF (EDLI)

USER QUERY:
Left my job 2 months back, 4 years service. UAN active, KYC complete. Full PF withdrawal karna hai.

DOMAIN: pf

RETRIEVED PASSAGES:
[Source 1] doc_id=FAQ_CLAIM_122 | date=2024-01-01 | domain=pf
Q: What are the different types of non-refundable advances? A: Non-refundable advances are available under: Para 62 (Life Insurance), Para 68-B/BC/BD (Purchase of House/Flat), Para 68-BB (Repayment of housing loan), Para 68-H (Factory closure), Para 68HH (Unemployment), Para 68-J (Illness), Para 68-K (Marriage/Education), Para 68-L (Natural calamity), Para 68-M (Electricity cut), Para 68-N (Physically handicapped), Para 68-NN (Pre-retirement at 54+), Para 68-NNN (Varishta Pension Bima Yojana). Eligibility and amount limits vary per provision.

---

[Source 2] doc_id=TDS_PRACTICAL_GUIDE_2024_chunk_1 | date=2024-04-01 | domain=pf
(basic exemption for senior citizens). 2024 UPDATES — KEY CHANGES: 1. TDS on Transfer NOT applicable: Transfer of PF from one account to another is not treated as withdrawal — no TDS even if service < 5 years. 2. Advances/Partial withdrawals (Form 31): TDS is NOT applicable on partial withdrawal advances. 3. Split accounts (EPF + VPF): TDS calculated on total taxable withdrawal, not split separately. 4. Non-resident (NRI) members: TDS at 30% (or DTAA rate if lower) — Form 15G/15H not available to NRIs. File Form 10F for DTAA benefit. WHAT HAPPENS AFTER TDS DEDUCTION: - Form 16A (TDS certificate) is issued by EPFO within 15 days from due date of TDS return - TDS can be claimed as credit in your Income Tax Return (ITR) - If your total income is below taxable limit, you can claim TDS refund from Income Tax Department EXAMPLE SCENARIOS: Scenario A: Service 4 years, balance Rs.80,000, valid PAN → TDS = 10% of Rs.80,000 = Rs.8,000. Submit Form 15G to avoid if income < Rs.2.5 lakh. Scenario B: Service 4 years, balance Rs.40,000, valid PAN → NO TDS (below Rs.50,000 threshold). Scenario C: Service 6 years, balance Rs.2,00,000 → NO TDS (service >= 5 years). Scenario D: Service 3 years, no PAN → TDS = 20% on amount above threshold. TAX YEAR NOTE: For withdrawal spanning two financial years, TDS is calculated in the year of withdrawal. Always provide latest Form 15G for the current financial year (April to March).

---

[Source 3] doc_id=INOPERATIVE_ACCOUNTS | date=2024-01-01 | domain=pf
An EPF account is classified as Inoperative when no contribution has been received for 3 years after:
        - Retirement
        - Permanent migration abroad
        - Death
        
        INTEREST ON INOPERATIVE ACCOUNTS:
        - At present, all accounts earn interest up to age 58 of the member
        - After account becomes inoperative, no further interest is credited
        - Account becomes inoperative after 36 months from date of retirement (55 years+) or permanent migration
        
        WHAT TO DO WITH INOPERATIVE ACCOUNT:
        - If still employed in covered establishment: Get amount transferred to new account (online or offline Form 13)
        - If retired: Withdraw the amount by submitting withdrawal claim
        
        UNCLAIMED PF (SENIOR CITIZENS FUND):
        Unclaimed EPF amounts are transferred to Senior Citizens Welfare Fund (SCWF) after 7 years of becoming inoperative. However, members can still claim this amount from EPFO.
        
        HOW TO CLAIM INOPERATIVE ACCOUNT:
        - Trace the account using establishment name or PF number
        - Contact the employer for details
        - Submit withdrawal claim to concerned Regional PF Office with proper KYC documentation
        
        PASSBOOK FOR INOPERATIVE ACCOUNTS:
        - For exempted establishments: Passbook not visible on UAN portal; contact establishment directly
        - For non-exempted: Login to UAN portal passbook section

---

[Source 4] doc_id=CIRC_2024_TDS | date=2024-02-01 | domain=pf
TDS applicable if service < 5 years AND withdrawal exceeds Rs.50,000. Resident rate with valid PAN: 10%. Resident without valid PAN: 20%. Non-resident: 30% plus 4% cess. No TDS if service >= 5 years or transfer of funds.

}

        PASSBOOK NOT AVAILABLE:
        If working in an Exempted establishment, passbook is not available on UAN portal. Contact establishment directly for PF statement.
        
        INTEREST CREDITING:

        - Credited annually after Central Government declares rate
        - Delay in passbook update does not cause financial loss — interest is added to closing balance and becomes opening balance for next year

        SERVICE HISTORY:
        Login to e-Passbook > Click on Service History to see employment details across multiple establishments.
